title: clerodendrum parvulum
intro: Clerodendrum parvulum, also known as the small glory bower or small glorybower, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae, native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its leaves and flowers are valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Clerodendrum parvulum include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and providing relief from skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it easily accessible for those looking to harness its medicinal properties.
